understanding.goal_understanding: The goal of the game was to move a pushable block (B) onto the goal tile(s) (+) using the available selectable pieces (P, S) within a limited number of moves, as indicated by the semantic grid and the game state. The game was complete when the win condition—typically the block on the goal—was satisfied and the engine reported WIN.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Movement involved shifting the currently selected piece by one cell (3 pixels) per move. Pushing occurred when moving into a pushable block from an adjacent position, causing it to move along if space allowed. Walls (#) blocked movement directionally, with some variance in their behavior that required testing. Selection was switched between pieces by issuing a CLICK command with the pixel position of another selectable piece, allowing for coordinated actions to solve the puzzle.
